steps.title: Spot the opening parenthesis
steps.content: The sentence reads: "'cinch' (a ______ comes from cincho." An opening parenthesis appears after "cinch," so the blank must supply the definition of cinch AND a closing parenthesis to balance the opening one.

steps.title: Choose the closing parenthesis
steps.content:
Rule: every opening parenthesis must be closed before returning to the main sentence. Test each reconstructed sentence:

A) belt; → "cinch (a belt; comes from cincho" — the parenthesis is never closed, and a semicolon inside an unclosed parenthetical breaks the sentence. Fails.

B) belt) → "cinch (a belt) comes from cincho" — the parenthesis is properly closed immediately after the definition word, and the main sentence continues cleanly. Correct.

C) belt— → "cinch (a belt— comes from cincho" — the parenthesis is never closed; the em dash inside an unclosed parenthetical creates an unresolvable structure. Fails.

D) belt, → "cinch (a belt, comes from cincho" — the parenthesis is never closed; the comma only adds a pause inside the unclosed parenthetical. Fails.
